title: lithocarpus megalophyllus
intro: Lithocarpus megalophyllus, also known as the Giant Bur oak, is a species of evergreen tree native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its large, leathery leaves and broad, rounded crown. Traditionally, Lithocarpus megalophyllus has been used in herbal medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Lithocarpus megalophyllus include reducing fever, relieving rheumatism and arthritis symptoms, and supporting immune function. Lithocarpus megalophyllus is commonly prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture, and can also be used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
